items.title: Bethesda officially confirms Fallout 3 and New Vegas remasters + all-new Fallout game from Obsidian, more
items.summary: Things have been rough over at Xbox lately with mass layoffs spanning some of its biggest studios, id Software, Bethesda, and Obsidian included. But it’s not all doom and gloom as Bethesda has just confirmed a number of new projects in the works, including an all-new Fallout game from Obsidian, as well as remasters for Fallout 3 and Fallout: New Vegas.

items.title: Amazon’s pre-order deal on Splatoon Raiders now live again ahead of Switch 2 release next week
items.summary:
Now under a week from release, Splatoon Raiders is set to splat onto Nintendo Switch 2 next week come July 23, and Amazon’s pre-order discount is now back in-stock. After disappearing for a short earlier this week, Nintendo is once again offering physical copies of the upcoming single-player Splatoon experience down at $10 off the list price to give folks a chance to and a proper copy for the game shelf at a discount ahead of release. You will also find this deal live at Walmart if Amazon sells out again – these pre-order deals on new Switch 2 games have all disappeared as soon as the game releases, if not shortly beforehand.

  • Grand Theft Auto VI pre-orders $80
  • Splatoon Raiders $50 (Reg. $60)
  • Switch Sports Resort $50 (Reg. $60) | Walmart
  • Switch Sports Resort $50 (Reg. $60) | Amazon
  • Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave Nintendo Switch 2 Edition $70 (Reg. $80)
  • Orbitals Nintendo Switch 2 Edition $40 (Reg. $50)

Buy for $13.50 (Reg. $20)

What we love

At just $13, this Amazon charging pad is an excellent deal for a Qi2.2-certified charger with support for up to 25W wireless charging. It snaps magnetically onto MagSafe-compatible iPhones and supported Android devices, and it even comes with a 5-foot cord.

items.title: Native Union’s 240W USB-C Belt Cable Pro with leather strap starts from $18 today
items.summary:
Native Union 240W USB-C Belt Cable Pro with leather strap

Buy for $18 (Reg. $29)

What we love

There’s nothing wrong with the good bargain-bin cables (most of the time) but I like to have a couple really nice-looking USB-C cables on hand. Whether it’s something I have to see everyday, or to pull out of my EDC during meetings and the like, something like this Native Union Type-C Belt Cable Pro that delivers 240W of power and includes a leather organizer strap with a button closure is a solid option – I particularly like the Kraft and Zebra colorways too.
